# Quantum computing benchmarking
**Most quantum benchmarks report hardware specs and small tests. IonQ's framework measures whether a quantum system solves real computational problems accurately, against a verified quality standard.**
Results are standardized, publicly reproducible, and compared across systems on equal terms. For easy reproducibility on different Quantum Computing platforms, benchmarks, and their code are publicly available at the link below.
[Read white paper](https://www.ionq.com/resources/benchmarking-white-paper)[View repository](http://github.com/ionq-publications/apps-benchmark)
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69d95dd990f5ee38073fa54c_benchmarking.png)
## Core benchmark principles
Inspired by leading AI benchmarking frameworks like MLPerf and DAWNBench, our benchmark framework evaluates the complete technical stack and makes comparisons that are meaningful to the people and businesses that rely on it.
###### Full system stack evaluation
Each benchmark evaluates the complete system: hardware, compiler, runtime, and all performance management layers (error mitigation, error correction, and circuit optimizations). Component-level metrics matter, but they do not capture the trade-offs that come with every architectural decision. Higher gate fidelity can mean slower gate speed. Error correction consumes physical qubits and increases circuit depth. Application benchmarks measure how the entire system performs together on a real problem.
###### Reproducible and transparent
This framework follows MLPerf’s structure, with two categories of benchmarks. Closed benchmarks fix the implementation so results are directly comparable across platforms and vendors (the code is “closed” to modifications). Open benchmarks fix the success criterion but allow algorithmic innovation, so teams can show their best results without disclosing proprietary methods (it is “open” to new code). Both the quantum and classical code for “closed” benchmarks is available in Qiskit on GitHub for independent verification.
###### Measuring time and quality
Every benchmark in this suite reports a solution quality score as a metric. When measuring quality, all answers are accepted and critically compared to results from other systems. When measuring Time-to-Solution, an answer only counts if it meets a predefined accuracy threshold, and time measurement continues until the correct answer is provided (which could also be unattainable - infinite TTS). Energy-to-Solution (ETS) and Cost-to-Solution (CTS) are the outcomes of each benchmark and can be compared head-to-head across systems.
## How performance is scored
Most quantum benchmarks today report hardware specs: qubit count, gate fidelity, gate speed, and error rates. Our approach tells you what a system is actually capable of in terms of the quality and time-to-solution of relevant, real-world computational problems.
Every benchmark in this suite is scored on quality. When measuring Time-to-Solution (TTS), what counts as a correct answer is defined before any system runs. An answer that misses the bar does not count, and time measurement continues.  
  
TTS benchmarks measure the total wall time from job submission to a verified, qualifying answer. This covers the full pipeline from compilation through post-processing. Both types require a great underlying system. The difference is whether we measure time or quality as the output.  
  
Results are reported per workload. A single aggregated benchmarking score cannot represent performance across problems that differ in structure, depth, and required accuracy.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd2a69fb9d3b0d2f34c83a_Group%20114.png)
### Solution Quality
Each benchmark defines upfront what a correct or ideal/optimal answer looks like. For a chemistry simulation, the result has to land within a precise energy range of the known correct molecular energy. For an optimization problem, the solution has to come within a defined percentage of the theoretical best. For a machine learning task, the model has to hit a minimum accuracy threshold. The bar is set before any system runs. A result that misses it is not counted, regardless of how fast it was produced.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd2aace565f99316e653a7_Group%20112.png)
### Time-to-Solution
The clock starts at job submission and stops when a verified, qualifying answer is returned, or the last shot is completed. Compilation, quantum execution, error mitigation, and all classical co-processing count toward the total. Because many benchmarks in this suite involve hybrid quantum-classical workflows, classical co-processing time is included by design. TTS is reported for the five benchmarks in which the problem structure supports a meaningful, reproducible time comparison across systems.
## Real-world results:  
IonQ vs peers
[ TTS ]
### LR-QAOA
Linear-Ramp QAOA
IonQ’s system returns qualifying answers in seconds, even at the highest quality threshold (orange area). At that same quality bar, competing superconducting systems produce results statistically indistinguishable from random sampling, meaning the answers they return carry no useful signal and their Time-to-Solution can be considered “infinite”.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dbd5e6bc96e3038803a16f_LR-QAOA-graph.png)
[ TTS ]
### QFT Suite
Quantum Fourier Transform Suite
IonQ’s system maintains accuracy close to the ideal result across all tested circuit sizes. Competing systems degrade faster as circuits grow. At the largest sizes (orange area), the competing system’s scores approach what you would expect from a system dominated by noise, and Time-to-Solution becomes impossibly high.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dbd6cab4f1462a9f121a11_QFT-graph.png)
[ TTS ]
### HSBP
Hidden Shift Benchmark Problem
IonQ’s systems return qualifying answers across the full range of quality requirements, including the hardest targets (orange area). Competing systems fail to produce valid results before reaching the highest bar, meaning they cannot complete this benchmark at the required standard for this problem size.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dbd6fd0b3a08d162579d94_HSBP-graph.png)
[ SOLUTION QUALITY ]
### VQE
Variational Quantum Eigensolver
This benchmark scores solution quality rather than speed. The chart shows how far each system's answer drifts from the correct molecular energy as the problem grows. IonQ stays closest to that target across all molecule sizes. Competing systems diverge more steeply as complexity increases.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dbd75fedcf4478df637c1c_VQE-graph.png)
## The quantum benchmark suite
Application-level benchmarks across optimization, quantum chemistry, machine learning, data loading, simulation, and other foundational subroutines.
This benchmarking suite was designed to allow for new benchmark challenges to be proposed and launched as new application domains emerge, and existing problems can be modified or retired as hardware capabilities and relevant business problems shift.  
  
Each benchmark’s code, which is designed to be fixed (the “closed” category), is publicly available for independent verification, while benchmarks in the “open” category are open for innovation, and other companies can come up with novel ways to address the benchmark challenge in the best way possible.

An alternative quantum approach to optimization that runs once, with no iterative classical feedback. The circuit is pre-determined and runs to completion. Image Loading via MPS

The score reveals where and how fast that accumulation happens.

Quantum Fourier Transform Suite
The Quantum Fourier Transform is central to some of quantum computing's most important algorithms, including those for cryptography and chemistry. The problem is that existing QFT benchmarks are often trivially simplified by modern compilers, producing scores that do not reflect real-world execution. This suite closes that gap: each variant is specifically designed so that the full circuit must execute.

Neutrinoless Double Beta Decay Simulation
This is a physics simulation run at the edge of what current hardware can execute. Noise accumulating across hundreds of operations is the dominant challenge at this scale. The benchmark asks whether the hardware can still recover a statistically meaningful signal from that level of complexity, a direct test of what present-day systems can do at scale.

Quantum-Classical Auxiliary Field Quantum Monte Carlo
The quantum computer prepares an initial state that gives a classical simulation a better starting point, allowing it to compute molecular energies more accurately than it could on its own. The hybrid workflow is timed from start to finish. The score is whether the combined system reaches a chemically accurate answer, and how long that takes.

### How IonQ designed its quantum performance framework
Most quantum benchmarks today report hardware specs: qubit count, gate fidelity, gate speed, and error rates. Our approach tells you what a system is actually capable of in terms of the quality and time-to-solution of relevant, real-world computational problems.

white paper

</style></div><div class="hidden-js w-embed w-script"><script>
  if (window.innerWidth >= 992) {
    document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function() {
      var navRoot = document.querySelector('.navigation');
      var navBg = document.querySelector('.nav-dd-list-bg');
      var navOverlay = document.querySelector('.nav-close-overlay');
      var navDropdowns = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.nav-dropdown.w-dropdown'));

      var navOverlayDelayTimeout = null;
      var navIsSwitching = false;
      var navOverlayIsVisible = false;

      function navGetListAndInner(navDropdown) {
        var navList = navDropdown.querySelector('.w-dropdown-list');
        if (!navList) return { list: null, inner: null };
        var navInner = navList.firstElementChild || navList;
        return { list: navList, inner: navInner };
      }

      function navAnyOpen() {
        return navDropdowns.some(function(navDD) {
          var pair = navGetListAndInner(navDD);
          return pair.list && pair.list.classList.contains('w--open');
        });
      }

      function navShowOverlayImmediate() {
        if (!navOverlay) return;
        if (navOverlayIsVisible) return;
        navOverlayIsVisible = true;
        navOverlay.style.display = "block";
        navOverlay.style.opacity = "0";
        requestAnimationFrame(function() {
          navOverlay.style.transition = "opacity 250ms ease";
          navOverlay.style.opacity = "1";
        });
      }

      function navHideOverlayImmediate() {
        if (!navOverlay) return;
        navOverlay.style.transition = "opacity 250ms ease";
        navOverlay.style.opacity = "0";
        setTimeout(function() {
          navOverlay.style.display = "none";
          navOverlayIsVisible = false;
        }, 250);
      }

      function navUpdateOverlay(triggeredFromClick) {
        clearTimeout(navOverlayDelayTimeout);
        var openExists = navAnyOpen();

        if (!openExists) {
          if (navIsSwitching) {
            navOverlayDelayTimeout = setTimeout(function() {
              if (!navAnyOpen()) {
                navHideOverlayImmediate();
                if (navRoot) {
                  navRoot.classList.remove('open');
                }
                navIsSwitching = false;
              }
            }, 170);
          } else {
            navHideOverlayImmediate();
            if (navRoot) {
              navRoot.classList.remove('open');
            }
            navIsSwitching = false;
          }
          return;
        }

        navIsSwitching = false;

        if (navRoot) {
          navRoot.classList.add('open');
        }

        if (triggeredFromClick === true) {
          navShowOverlayImmediate();
        }
      }

      function navUpdateBgHeight() {
        var navOpen = null;

        navDropdowns.forEach(function(navDD) {
          var pair = navGetListAndInner(navDD);
          if (pair.list && pair.list.classList.contains('w--open')) {
            navOpen = pair;
          }
        });

        if (!navOpen) {
          if (navBg) {
            navBg.style.height = '0px';
          }
          navUpdateOverlay(false);
          return;
        }

        var h1 = navOpen.inner.scrollHeight;
        var h2 = navRoot ? navRoot.offsetHeight || 0 : 0;
        if (navBg) {
          navBg.style.height = (h1 + h2) + 'px';
        }
        navUpdateOverlay(true);
      }

      navDropdowns.forEach(function(navDD) {
        var navToggle = navDD.querySelector('.w-dropdown-toggle');
        if (!navToggle) return;

        navToggle.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
          e.stopPropagation();
          var anyOpenNow = navAnyOpen();
          var pair = navGetListAndInner(navDD);
          var thisIsOpen = pair.list && pair.list.classList.contains('w--open');
          if (anyOpenNow && !thisIsOpen) {
            navIsSwitching = true;
          } else {
            navIsSwitching = false;
          }
        });

        $(window).on("scroll", function () {
          var pair = navGetListAndInner(navDD);
          if (pair.list && pair.list.classList.contains("w--open")) {
            $(navDD).trigger('w-close.w-dropdown');
            if (navBg) {
              navBg.style.height = '0px';
            }
            clearTimeout(navOverlayDelayTimeout);
            navHideOverlayImmediate();
            if (navRoot) {
              navRoot.classList.remove('open');
            }
            navIsSwitching = false;
          }
        });
      });

      navDropdowns.forEach(function(navDD) {
        var pair = navGetListAndInner(navDD);
        if (!pair.list) return;

        var observer = new MutationObserver(function() {
          navUpdateBgHeight();
        });

        observer.observe(pair.list, { attributes: true, attributeFilter: ['class', 'style'] });
      });

      if (navOverlay) {
        navOverlay.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
          e.stopPropagation();
          navDropdowns.forEach(function(navDD) {
            $(navDD).trigger('w-close.w-dropdown');
          });
          if (navBg) {
            navBg.style.height = '0px';
          }
          clearTimeout(navOverlayDelayTimeout);
          navHideOverlayImmediate();
          if (navRoot) {
            navRoot.classList.remove('open');
          }
          navIsSwitching = false;
        });
      }

      document.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
        if (!navAnyOpen()) return;
        if (!e.target.closest('.nav-dropdown')) {
          navDropdowns.forEach(function(navDD) {
            $(navDD).trigger('w-close.w-dropdown');
          });
          if (navBg) {
            navBg.style.height = '0px';
          }
          clearTimeout(navOverlayDelayTimeout);
          navHideOverlayImmediate();
          if (navRoot) {
            navRoot.classList.remove('open');
          }
          navIsSwitching = false;
        }
      });

    });
  }
</script>

<script>
  if (window.innerWidth <= 991) {
    document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function() {
      var navRoot = document.querySelector('.navigation');
      var navMenuButton = document.querySelector('.nav-menu-button');
      if (!navRoot || !navMenuButton) return;

      navMenuButton.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
        e.stopPropagation();
        navRoot.classList.toggle('open');
      });
    });

</script></div><div class="gated-content-js w-embed w-script"><script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function () {
  const COOKIE_PREFIX = "__Secure-Session-US-Web";
  const COOKIE_DOMAIN = ".ionq.com";

  function getCookieByPrefix(prefix) {
    const row = document.cookie
      .split(";")
      .map(r => r.trim())
      .find(r => r.startsWith(prefix));
    return row ? decodeURIComponent(row.split("=")[1]) : null;
  }

  function getCookieNameByPrefix(prefix) {
    const row = document.cookie
      .split(";")
      .map(r => r.trim())
      .find(r => r.startsWith(prefix));
    return row ? row.split("=")[0] : null;
  }

  function deleteIonqSessionCookie() {
    const actualCookieName = getCookieNameByPrefix(COOKIE_PREFIX);
    if (!actualCookieName) return;

    document.cookie =
      actualCookieName +
      "=; Domain=" +
      COOKIE_DOMAIN +
      "; Path=/; Max-Age=0; Secure; SameSite=None";
  }

  async function isIonqUserLoggedIn() {
    const token = getCookieByPrefix(COOKIE_PREFIX);
    if (!token) return false;

    try {
      const res = await fetch("https://api.ionq.co/v0.3/users/me", {
        headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` }
      });

      if (res.status !== 200) return false;

      const user = await res.json();
      return !!(user && user.confirmed === true);
    } catch (e) {
      return false;
    }
  }

  function applyAuthVisibility(isLoggedIn) {
    document.querySelectorAll('[auth="off"]').forEach(el => {
      el.style.setProperty("display", isLoggedIn ? "none" : "flex", "important");
    });

    document.querySelectorAll('[auth="on"]').forEach(el => {
      el.style.setProperty("display", isLoggedIn ? "flex" : "none", "important");
    });
  }

  const signOutBtn = document.querySelector('[auth-btn="sign-out"]');
  if (signOutBtn) {
    signOutBtn.addEventListener("click", function () {
      deleteIonqSessionCookie();
      applyAuthVisibility(false);
    });
  }

  isIonqUserLoggedIn().then(isLoggedIn => {
    applyAuthVisibility(isLoggedIn);
  });
});
</script></div><div class="w-nav-overlay" data-wf-ignore="" id="w-nav-overlay-0"></div></div><div class="main-wrapper"><section class="hero-section legal"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer container grid z-99 w-container"><div id="w-node-fc087fb2-b321-944d-54e8-b3f519aaf0d6-8bd842b2" class="column max-width-7c benchmark-hero"><h1>Quantum computing benchmarking</h1><div class="margin-top-m"><strong class="bold-text">Most quantum benchmarks report hardware specs and small tests. IonQ's framework measures whether a quantum system solves real computational problems accurately, against a verified quality standard.</strong></div><div class="margin-top-m">Results are standardized, publicly reproducible, and compared across systems on equal terms. For easy reproducibility on different Quantum Computing platforms, benchmarks, and their code are publicly available at the link below.</div><div class="button-wrapper margin-top-l"><a href="https://www.ionq.com/resources/benchmarking-white-paper" class="button w-button">Read white paper</a><a href="http://github.com/ionq-publications/apps-benchmark" target="_blank" class="button-tertiary w-button">View repository</a></div></div><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69d95dd990f5ee38073fa54c_benchmarking.png" loading="lazy" id="w-node-_9f5f44ed-bab2-e685-3ec6-8b4d61d344b1-8bd842b2" sizes="(max-width: 631px) 100vw, 631px" alt="" srcset="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69d95dd990f5ee38073fa54c_benchmarking-p-500.png 500w, https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69d95dd990f5ee38073fa54c_benchmarking.png 631w" class="full-stack-hero-image" width="631" height="573"></div></section><section class="section bg-black"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer container w-container"><div class="w-row"><div class="w-col w-col-9"><h2 class="heading-7">Core benchmark <span class="text-span-3">principles</span></h2><div class="text-block-20 max-width-7c">Inspired by leading AI benchmarking frameworks like MLPerf and DAWNBench, our benchmark framework evaluates the complete technical stack and makes comparisons that are meaningful to the people and businesses that rely on it.</div></div><div class="column-2 w-col w-col-3"></div></div><div class="w-row"><div class="column-5 w-col w-col-4"><h6 class="heading-4">Full system stack evaluation</h6><div class="text-block">Each benchmark evaluates the complete system: hardware, compiler, runtime, and all performance management layers (error mitigation, error correction, and circuit optimizations). Component-level metrics matter, but they do not capture the trade-offs that come with every architectural decision. Higher gate fidelity can mean slower gate speed. Error correction consumes physical qubits and increases circuit depth. Application benchmarks measure how the entire system performs together on a real problem.</div></div><div class="column-3 w-col w-col-4"><h6 class="heading-5">Reproducible and transparent</h6><div class="text-block-2">This framework follows MLPerf’s structure, with two categories of benchmarks. Closed benchmarks fix the implementation so results are directly comparable across platforms and vendors (the code is “closed” to modifications). Open benchmarks fix the success criterion but allow algorithmic innovation, so teams can show their best results without disclosing proprietary methods (it is “open” to new code). Both the quantum and classical code for “closed” benchmarks is available in Qiskit on GitHub for independent verification.</div></div><div class="column-4 w-col w-col-4"><h6 class="heading-6">Measuring time and quality</h6><div class="text-block-3">Every benchmark in this suite reports a solution quality score as a metric. When measuring quality, all answers are accepted and critically compared to results from other systems. When measuring Time-to-Solution, an answer only counts if it meets a predefined accuracy threshold, and time measurement continues until the correct answer is provided (which could also be unattainable - infinite TTS). Energy-to-Solution (ETS) and Cost-to-Solution (CTS) are the outcomes of each benchmark and can be compared head-to-head across systems.</div></div></div></div></section><section class="section bg-ionq-black"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer container w-container"><div class="div-block-12"><div class="performance-is-scored"><div class="div-block-11"><h2 class="heading-8">How <span class="text-span-4">performance</span> <span class="text-span-6">is scored</span></h2><div class="text-block-23">Most quantum benchmarks today report hardware specs: qubit count, gate fidelity, gate speed, and error rates. Our approach tells you what a system is actually capable of in terms of the quality and time-to-solution of relevant, real-world computational problems.</div><div class="text-block-24">Every benchmark in this suite is scored on quality. When measuring Time-to-Solution (TTS), what counts as a correct answer is defined before any system runs. An answer that misses the bar does not count, and time measurement continues.<br><br>TTS benchmarks measure the total wall time from job submission to a verified, qualifying answer. This covers the full pipeline from compilation through post-processing. Both types require a great underlying system. The difference is whether we measure time or quality as the output.<br><br>Results are reported per workload. A single aggregated benchmarking score cannot represent performance across problems that differ in structure, depth, and required accuracy.</div></div></div><div class="solution-quality-and-tts max-width-6c"><div class="div-block-7"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd2a69fb9d3b0d2f34c83a_Group%20114.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-3"><h3 class="heading-9">Solution Quality</h3><div class="text-block-19">Each benchmark defines upfront what a correct or ideal/optimal answer looks like. For a chemistry simulation, the result has to land within a precise energy range of the known correct molecular energy. For an optimization problem, the solution has to come within a defined percentage of the theoretical best. For a machine learning task, the model has to hit a minimum accuracy threshold. The bar is set before any system runs. A result that misses it is not counted, regardless of how fast it was produced.</div></div><div class="div-block-8"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd2aace565f99316e653a7_Group%20112.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-3"><h3 class="heading-10">Time-to-Solution</h3><div class="text-block-19">The clock starts at job submission and stops when a verified, qualifying answer is returned, or the last shot is completed. Compilation, quantum execution, error mitigation, and all classical co-processing count toward the total. Because many benchmarks in this suite involve hybrid quantum-classical workflows, classical co-processing time is included by design. TTS is reported for the five benchmarks in which the problem structure supports a meaningful, reproducible time comparison across systems.</div></div></div></div></div></section><section class="section bg-black"><div class="home-products-animation-trigger"></div><div class="container"><div id="w-node-_973da6cf-6872-f104-135a-658881e9a9d7-8bd842b2" class="column home-products-title-column"><h2 class="text-white">Real-world results:<br><span class="text-span-2 text-span">IonQ vs peers</span></h2></div><div class="horizontal-divider"></div><div class="home-products-swiper"><div class="swiper-wrapper home-products-list"><div class="swiper-slide home-product-item first"><div class="home-products-column"><div class="label-s-2 text-tertiary-2">[ TTS ]</div><div class="home-products-content"><div class="column"><h3 class="text-white-2">LR-QAOA</h3><p class="text-tertiary-2 margin-2xl">Linear-Ramp QAOA</p><p class="text-tertiary-2 margin-3xl">IonQ’s system returns qualifying answers in seconds, even at the highest quality threshold (orange area). At that same quality bar, competing superconducting systems produce results statistically indistinguishable from random sampling, meaning the answers they return carry no useful signal and their Time-to-Solution can be considered “infinite”.</p></div></div></div><div class="home-product-slider-image-wrapper"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dbd5e6bc96e3038803a16f_LR-QAOA-graph.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image"></div></div><div class="swiper-slide home-product-item second"><div class="home-products-column"><div class="label-s-2 text-tertiary-2">[ TTS ]</div><div class="home-products-content"><div class="column"><h3 class="text-white-2">QFT Suite</h3><p class="text-tertiary-2 margin-2xl">Quantum Fourier Transform Suite</p><p class="text-tertiary-2 margin-3xl">IonQ’s system maintains accuracy close to the ideal result across all tested circuit sizes. Competing systems degrade faster as circuits grow. At the largest sizes (orange area), the competing system’s scores approach what you would expect from a system dominated by noise, and Time-to-Solution becomes impossibly high.</p></div></div></div><div class="home-product-slider-image-wrapper"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dbd6cab4f1462a9f121a11_QFT-graph.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image"></div></div><div class="swiper-slide home-product-item third"><div class="home-products-column"><div class="label-s-2 text-tertiary-2">[ TTS ]</div><div class="home-products-content"><div class="column"><h3 class="text-white-2">HSBP</h3><p class="text-tertiary-2 margin-2xl">Hidden Shift Benchmark Problem</p><p class="text-tertiary-2 margin-3xl">IonQ’s systems return qualifying answers across the full range of quality requirements, including the hardest targets (orange area). Competing systems fail to produce valid results before reaching the highest bar, meaning they cannot complete this benchmark at the required standard for this problem size.</p></div></div></div><div class="home-product-slider-image-wrapper"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dbd6fd0b3a08d162579d94_HSBP-graph.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image"></div></div><div class="swiper-slide home-product-item fourth"><div class="home-products-column"><div class="label-s-2 text-tertiary-2">[ SOLUTION QUALITY ]</div><div class="home-products-content"><div class="column"><h3 class="text-white-2">VQE</h3><p class="text-tertiary-2 margin-2xl">Variational Quantum Eigensolver</p><p class="text-tertiary-2 margin-3xl">This benchmark scores solution quality rather than speed. The chart shows how far each system's answer drifts from the correct molecular energy as the problem grows. IonQ stays closest to that target across all molecule sizes. Competing systems diverge more steeply as complexity increases.</p></div></div></div><div class="home-product-slider-image-wrapper"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dbd75fedcf4478df637c1c_VQE-graph.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image"></div></div></div></div></div></section><section That makes it a clean test: run the quantum circuit, compare the result to ground truth, and the gap tells you exactly how much noise the hardware introduced.</div><div class="collection-list-wrapper-3 w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="collection-list-3 w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Pharmaceuticals</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Materials</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Energy</div></div></div></div></div><div class="example-use-cases"><div class="text-block-18">Example Use Cases:</div><div class="w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Catalyst design for strongly correlated electron systems</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Battery material discovery and drug development</div></div></div></div></div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-4 w-dyn-item"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer w-container"><div fs-list-field="subroutines" class="text-block-17">Optimization</div><div class="div-block-5"><div class="text-block-13">QAOA</div><div class="div-block-3"><div class="quality-tag">Quality<br></div></div></div><div class="text-block-12">Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm</div><div class="text-block-14">This tests how well a quantum system finds near-optimal solutions to combinatorial problems. This is the class of problems that appears throughout routing, scheduling, and resource allocation. Algorithm parameters are fixed in advance, so any difference in results between systems reflects the hardware, not the software choices made around it.</div><div class="collection-list-wrapper-3 w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="collection-list-3 w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Finance</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Logistics</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Manufacturing</div></div></div></div></div><div class="example-use-cases"><div class="text-block-18">Example Use Cases:</div><div class="w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Semiconductor chip design and layout optimization</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Financial network community detection</div></div></div></div></div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-4 w-dyn-item"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer w-container"><div fs-list-field="subroutines" class="text-block-17">Optimization</div><div class="div-block-5"><div class="text-block-13">LR-QAOA</div><div class="div-block-3"><div class="quality-tag">Quality<br></div><div class="tts-tag">TTS</div></div></div><div class="text-block-12">Linear-Ramp QAOA</div><div class="text-block-14">This is a version of the optimization benchmark QAOA where nothing can be tuned. The full algorithm is specified before the circuit runs. Because there are no adjustable parameters, no system can get a better score by optimizing around its hardware's weaknesses. What you see is what the system can do.</div><div class="collection-list-wrapper-3 w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="collection-list-3 w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Finance</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Logistics</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Manufacturing</div></div></div></div></div><div class="example-use-cases"><div class="text-block-18">Example Use Cases:</div><div class="w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Logistics routing and hub-and-spoke network optimization</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Supply chain scheduling and resource allocation</div></div></div></div></div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-4 w-dyn-item"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer w-container"><div fs-list-field="subroutines" class="text-block-17">Optimization</div><div class="div-block-5"><div class="text-block-13">varQITE</div><div class="div-block-3"><div class="quality-tag">Quality<br></div></div></div><div class="text-block-12">Variational Quantum Imaginary Time Evolution</div><div class="text-block-14">An alternative quantum approach to optimization that runs once, with no iterative classical feedback. The circuit is pre-determined and runs to completion. This removes the classical overhead that makes variational algorithms difficult to time and compare, revealing what the hardware itself contributes to solution quality.</div><div class="collection-list-wrapper-3 w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="collection-list-3 w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Finance</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Logistics</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Manufacturing</div></div></div></div></div><div class="example-use-cases"><div class="text-block-18">Example Use Cases:</div><div class="w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Portfolio construction and asset allocation</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Supply chain and operations research</div></div></div></div></div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-4 w-dyn-item"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer w-container"><div fs-list-field="subroutines" class="text-block-17">Foundational Subroutines</div><div class="div-block-5"><div class="text-block-13">FAA</div><div class="div-block-3"><div class="quality-tag">Quality<br></div></div></div><div class="text-block-12">Fixed Point Amplitude Amplification</div><div class="text-block-14">Quantum computers are expected to search large solution spaces faster than classical alternatives. This benchmark tests that ability under realistic conditions: you do not know in advance how many correct answers exist. That constraint matters because it reflects how real-world search problems work and breaks most standard quantum search algorithms.</div><div class="collection-list-wrapper-3 w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="collection-list-3 w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Technology</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">AI/ML</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Security</div></div></div></div></div><div class="example-use-cases"><div class="text-block-18">Example Use Cases:</div><div class="w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Search over large, noisy decision graphs</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Molecular dynamics for drug discovery and synthetic biology</div></div></div></div></div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-4 w-dyn-item"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer w-container"><div fs-list-field="subroutines" class="text-block-17">Machine Learning</div><div class="div-block-5"><div class="text-block-13">qCNN</div><div class="div-block-3"><div class="quality-tag">Quality<br></div></div></div><div class="text-block-12">Quantum Convolutional Neural Network</div><div class="text-block-14">This benchmark runs a pre-trained quantum neural network on image classification and scores both accuracy and inference speed. The system is tested on whether it delivers correct, consistent results without recalibration between runs.</div><div class="collection-list-wrapper-3 w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="collection-list-3 w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Healthcare</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Security</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">AI/ML</div></div></div></div></div><div class="example-use-cases"><div class="text-block-18">Example Use Cases:</div><div class="w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Tissue classification for digital pathology</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Anomaly detection in security image datasets</div></div></div></div></div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-4 w-dyn-item"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer w-container"><div fs-list-field="subroutines" class="text-block-17">Machine Learning</div><div class="div-block-5"><div class="text-block-13">Quantum Copula</div><div class="div-block-3"><div class="quality-tag">Quality<br></div></div></div><div class="text-block-12">Portfolio Risk Analysis</div><div class="text-block-14">This benchmark trains a quantum circuit to understand how a group of assets tends to move together, then uses that model to predict portfolio risk. Those risk predictions are scored against actual historical market data, not a simulation. It is one of the few quantum benchmarks grounded in real financial outcomes.</div><div class="collection-list-wrapper-3 w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="collection-list-3 w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Finance</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Insurance</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Investment Banking</div></div></div></div></div><div class="example-use-cases"><div class="text-block-18">Example Use Cases:</div><div class="w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Portfolio risk modeling and reserve optimization</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Risk-weighted asset allocation</div></div></div></div></div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-4 w-dyn-item"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer w-container"><div fs-list-field="subroutines" class="text-block-17">Data Loading</div><div class="div-block-5"><div class="text-block-13">Tensor Network Circuits</div><div class="div-block-3"><div class="quality-tag">Quality<br></div></div></div><div class="text-block-12">Image Loading via MPS</div><div class="text-block-14">Before a quantum computer can process classical data, that data has to be loaded into the system without significant loss. This benchmark tests how accurately a system can do that with image data, across two datasets, as the encoding process becomes more complex. Noise accumulates with complexity. The score reveals where and how fast that accumulation happens.</div><div class="collection-list-wrapper-3 w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="collection-list-3 w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">AI/ML</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Healthcare</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Manufacturing</div></div></div></div></div><div class="example-use-cases"><div class="text-block-18">Example Use Cases:</div><div class="w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Manufacturing defect via image analysis</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Medical imaging diagnostics and enhancement</div></div></div></div></div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-4 w-dyn-item"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer w-container"><div fs-list-field="subroutines" class="text-block-17">Foundational Subroutines</div><div class="div-block-5"><div class="text-block-13">QFT</div><div class="div-block-3"><div class="quality-tag">Quality<br></div><div class="tts-tag">TTS</div></div></div><div class="text-block-12">Quantum Fourier Transform Suite</div><div class="text-block-14">The Quantum Fourier Transform is central to some of quantum computing's most important algorithms, including those for cryptography and chemistry. The problem is that existing QFT benchmarks are often trivially simplified by modern compilers, producing scores that do not reflect real-world execution. This suite closes that gap: each variant is specifically designed so that the full circuit must execute.</div><div class="collection-list-wrapper-3 w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="collection-list-3 w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Cryptography</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Security</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Metrology</div></div></div></div></div><div class="example-use-cases"><div class="text-block-18">Example Use Cases:</div><div class="w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">RSA decryption using Shor’s algorithm</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Quantum signal processing and metrology</div></div></div></div></div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-4 w-dyn-item"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer w-container"><div fs-list-field="subroutines" class="text-block-17">Foundational Subroutines</div><div class="div-block-5"><div class="text-block-13">HSBP</div><div class="div-block-3"><div class="quality-tag">Quality<br></div><div class="tts-tag">TTS</div></div></div><div class="text-block-12">Hidden Shift Benchmark Problem</div><div class="text-block-14">A stress test where the difficulty scales continuously, making it possible to find exactly where a system's performance starts to degrade. Rather than a single pass or fail, this benchmark maps out a system's capability curve. That information is more useful for hardware comparison than any single number.</div><div class="collection-list-wrapper-3 w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="collection-list-3 w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Cryptography</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Security</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Research</div></div></div></div></div><div class="example-use-cases"><div class="text-block-18">Example Use Cases:</div><div class="w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Hardware characterization for cryptographic workloads</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Gate performance validation at scale</div></div></div></div></div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-4 w-dyn-item"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer w-container"><div fs-list-field="subroutines" class="text-block-17 w-dyn-bind-empty"></div><div class="div-block-5"><div class="text-block-13">High Energy Physics</div><div class="div-block-3"><div class="quality-tag">Quality<br></div><div class="tts-tag">TTS</div></div></div><div class="text-block-12">Neutrinoless Double Beta Decay Simulation</div><div class="text-block-14">This is a physics simulation run at the edge of what current hardware can execute. Noise accumulating across hundreds of operations is the dominant challenge at this scale. The benchmark asks whether the hardware can still recover a statistically meaningful signal from that level of complexity, a direct test of what present-day systems can do at scale.</div><div class="collection-list-wrapper-3 w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="collection-list-3 w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Research</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Defense</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">National Labs</div></div></div></div></div><div class="example-use-cases"><div class="text-block-18">Example Use Cases:</div><div class="w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Nuclear physics simulation beyond Standard Model constraints</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Many-body quantum dynamics research</div></div></div></div></div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-4 w-dyn-item"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer w-container"><div fs-list-field="subroutines" class="text-block-17">Quantum Chemistry</div><div class="div-block-5"><div class="text-block-13">QC-AFQMC</div><div class="div-block-3"><div class="quality-tag">Quality<br></div></div></div><div class="text-block-12">Quantum-Classical Auxiliary Field Quantum Monte Carlo</div><div class="text-block-14">The quantum computer prepares an initial state that gives a classical simulation a better starting point, allowing it to compute molecular energies more accurately than it could on its own. The hybrid workflow is timed from start to finish. The score is whether the combined system reaches a chemically accurate answer, and how long that takes.</div><div class="collection-list-wrapper-3 w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="collection-list-3 w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Pharmaceuticals</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Materials Science</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Energy</div></div></div></div></div><div class="example-use-cases"><div class="text-block-18">Example Use Cases:</div><div class="w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Drug discovery and protein-ligand binding</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">High-temperature superconductor research</div></div></div></div></div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-4 w-dyn-item"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer w-container"><div fs-list-field="subroutines" class="text-block-17">Simulation</div><div class="div-block-5"><div class="text-block-13">QLBM</div><div class="div-block-3"><div class="quality-tag">Quality<br></div></div></div><div class="text-block-12">Quantum Lattice Boltzmann Method</div><div class="text-block-14">This simulates how a fluid evolves, then checks at each step whether the result matches the known classical solution. Running the simulation forward through multiple iterations tests whether the hardware can sustain accuracy over a full sequence, not just on a single circuit.</div><div class="collection-list-wrapper-3 w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="collection-list-3 w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Engineering</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Climate</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-3 w-dyn-item"><div class="benchmark-tag">Materials Science</div></div></div></div></div><div class="example-use-cases"><div class="text-block-18">Example Use Cases:</div><div class="w-dyn-list"><div role="list" class="w-dyn-items"><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Fluid dynamics simulation for aerospace and automotive engineering</div></div><div role="listitem" class="collection-item-5 w-dyn-item"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68836cf9cff21bf9efa61419/69dd28881154eed3f2ec8b8a_68ebae53660119903f61d6be_Vector%2520(8).svg.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="image-2"><div class="text-block-22">Climate and atmospheric modeling</div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></section></div></section><section class="section bg-image"><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer container w-container"><div class="w-row"><div class="w-col w-col-4"><h3 class="heading-13">Behind the<br>benchmark:</h3></div><div class="column-7 w-col w-col-8"><h3 class="heading-14">How IonQ designed its <span class="text-span-7">quantum performance framework</span></h3><div class="text-block-25">Most quantum benchmarks today report hardware specs: qubit count, gate fidelity, gate speed, and error rates. Our approach tells you what a system is actually capable of in terms of the quality and time-to-solution of relevant, real-world computational problems.</div><div class="w-layout-blockcontainer container-5 w-container"><a href="http://www.ionq.com/blog/beyond-qubit-counts-introducing-ionqs-application-centric-benchmarking-framework" class="link-2">Read the post</a><div class="resources-card-button-icon w-embed"><svg width="100%" height="100%" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">

</svg></div></a></div></div></div><div class="hidden-js w-embed w-script"><script>
  // --------------------------------------------------------------------
  // MAIN SCRIPT
  // --------------------------------------------------------------------

  document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function () {


    // ####################################
    /**
 * Minimalni JSONP helper – isti princip kao u testMailchimp snippetu.
 * url: baza URL-a (može već da sadrži query parametre)
 * callbackParamName: npr. "c" za Mailchimp
 * cb: function (err, data)
 */
    function jsonp(url, callbackParamName, cb) {
      const callbackName =
            "mcJsonpCallback_" + Math.random().toString(36).substring(2, 10);

      const script = document.createElement("script");

      const separator = url.includes("?") ? "&" : "?";
      script.src = url + separator + callbackParamName + "=" + callbackName;

      window[callbackName] = function (data) {
        try {
          cb(null, data);
        } finally {
          delete window[callbackName];
          script.remove();
        }
      };

      script.onerror = function (err) {
        try {
          cb(err || new Error("JSONP network error"));
        } finally {
          delete window[callbackName];
          script.remove();
        }
      };

      document.body.appendChild(script);
    }

    /**
 * Šalje podatke iz forme ka Mailchimpu preko JSONP-a.
 * Očekuje:
 *  - <form mailchimp-url="...">
 *  - inpute sa [data-mailchimp="EMAIL"], [data-mailchimp="NAME"], itd.
 *
 * @param {HTMLFormElement} form
 * @returns {Promise<any>}  // resolve(data) iz Mailchimp-a
 */
    function sendMailchimpFromForm(form) {
      if (!form) {
        return Promise.reject(new Error("Form element nije prosleđen."));
      }

      const mailchimpURL = form.getAttribute("mailchimp-url");
      if (!mailchimpURL) {
        return Promise.reject(
          new Error('Form nema "mailchimp-url" atribut.')
        );
      }

      // Skupi sve fieldove koji imaju data-mailchimp atribut
      const fields = form.querySelectorAll("[data-mailchimp]");

      if (!fields.length) {
        console.error(
          "❌ Form nema nijedan element sa [data-mailchimp] atributom.",
          form
        );
        return Promise.reject(
          new Error("Form nema nijedan [data-mailchimp] field.")
        );
      }

      // Napravi params objekat iz fieldova
      const params = {};
      fields.forEach((field) => {
        const key = field.getAttribute("data-mailchimp"); // npr. "EMAIL", "NAME"
        if (!key) return;

        const value =
              field.value != null
        ? field.value
        : field.getAttribute("value") || "";

        params[key] = value;
      });

      console.log("📝 Mailchimp params iz forme:", params);

      // Umesto toQueryString – koristimo URLSearchParams direktno
      const searchParams = new URLSearchParams();
      Object.keys(params).forEach((key) => {
        const val = params[key];
        if (val != null && val !== "") {
          searchParams.append(key, val);
        }
      });

      const queryString = searchParams.toString();
      if (!queryString) {
        console.error("❌ Query string je prazan iako postoje fieldovi.");
        return Promise.reject(
          new Error("Nema nijednog validnog data-mailchimp parametra.")
        );
      }

      // mailchimpURL već treba da sadrži ?u=...&id=...
      const separator = mailchimpURL.includes("?") ? "&" : "?";
      const requestUrl = mailchimpURL + separator + queryString;

      console.log("🌐 Mailchimp request URL:", requestUrl);

      return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
        jsonp(
          requestUrl,
          "c", // Mailchimp callback param, kao u prvom snippetu
          (err, data) => {
            if (err) {
              console.error("Mailchimp JSONP error:", err);
              reject(err);
            } else {
              // console.log("Mailchimp response:", data);
              resolve(data);
            }
          }
        );
      });
    }

    //######################################

    const DEBUG_MODE = true;

    function log(message, data) {
      if (DEBUG_MODE) console.log(message, data || "");
    }

    // --- UTILS ---

    function getGoogleAnalyticsClientId() {
      try {
        const match = document.cookie.match(/_ga=GA1\.1\.(\d+\.\d+)/);
        return match ? match[1] : null;
      } catch (e) {
        return null;
      }
    }

    function getReferrerIds(queryString) {
      const params = new URLSearchParams(queryString);
      const keys = [
        "utm_source",
        "utm_medium",
        "utm_campaign",
        "utm_content",
        "utm_term",
        "gclid",
        "fbclid",
        "msclkid",
        "li_fat_id"
      ];
      const result = {};
      keys.forEach((key) => {
        if (params.has(key)) result[key] = params.get(key);
      });
      return result;
    }

    // --- INTEGRACIJA SA formState (validation handler) ---

    const trackedForms = document.querySelectorAll('form[data-tracker="true"]');

    // Ako formState ne postoji (validation skripta nije učitana) → ne radimo ništa
    if (typeof formState === "undefined") {
      log("[Segment handler] formState nije definisan, preskačem registraciju onComplete handlera.");
    } else {
      trackedForms.forEach((form) => {
        const func = () => {
          // code
          handleFormSubmit(form);
          //############################
          //funkcija za mailchimp
          const mailchimpUrl = form.getAttribute("mailchimp-url");
          if (mailchimpUrl && mailchimpUrl.trim() !== "") {
            sendMailchimpFromForm(form)
              .then((data) => {
              console.log("✅ Mailchimp response:", data);
            })
              .catch((err) => {
              console.error("❌ Greška pri slanju ka Mailchimpu:", err);
            });
          }
          // ###########################
        };
        // email-form-1 == form ID
        formState[form.id] = {
          onComplete: func
        };
        log("[Segment handler] Registered formState handler for:", form.id);
      });
    }

    // --- GLAVNA LOGIKA ---

    function handleFormSubmit(form) {
      log("Form submit detected. Processing...", form);

      const segmentIdentify = {};
      const segmentTrack = {};

      const gaId = getGoogleAnalyticsClientId();
      const formNameAttr = form.getAttribute("data-form-name");
      const formMode = form.getAttribute("data-form-mode") || "track";
      const urlMode = form.getAttribute("data-url-mode") || "track";
      const eventName =
            form.getAttribute("data-segment-event") || "Form Submit";

      const minimalSegment =
            form.getAttribute("data-segment-minimal") === "true";
      const shouldIdentify =
            form.getAttribute("data-segment-identify") !== "false";

      const disableFormUrl =
            form.getAttribute("data-segment-url") === "disabled";

      const inputs = form.querySelectorAll("input, select, textarea");

      inputs.forEach((input) => {
        // 0) Globalni skip + submit dugmad
        if (
          input.type === "submit" ||
          input.getAttribute("data-skip") === "true"
        )
          return;

        // 1) vrednost (posebno za checkbox/radio)
        let value = input.value;

        const segmentOverride = input.getAttribute("data-segment-value");
        if (segmentOverride !== null) {
          value = segmentOverride;
        }

        if (input.type === "checkbox" || input.type === "radio") {
          if (!input.checked) return; // ne šaljemo unchecked
          if (segmentOverride === null) {
            value = input.value || true;
          }
        }
        if (value == null || value === "") return;

        const rawName = input.name || input.id || "unknown_field";

        // SEGMENT kontrola
        const segmentMode = input.getAttribute("data-segment-mode") || "both";
        const segmentKey =
              input.getAttribute("data-segment-key") || rawName;

        // 2) Specijalni slučajevi – PRE generičke logike
        const identity = input.getAttribute("data-identity");
        const isEmail = identity === "email" || input.type === "email";

        // 2.1 Marketing consent
        if (input.getAttribute("data-consent") === "marketing") {
          segmentIdentify["marketing_contact"] = "Marketing contact";
          return;
        }

        // 2.2 Full name
        if (identity === "full_name") {
          const parts = value.trim().split(/\s+/);
          const fName = parts.shift() || "";
          const lName = parts.join(" ");

          const fullNameMode = input.getAttribute("data-segment-mode") || "identify";
          if (fullNameMode !== "none") {
            if (fullNameMode === "identify" || fullNameMode === "both") {
              segmentIdentify["first_name"] = fName;
              if (lName) {
                segmentIdentify["last_name"] = lName;
              }
            }
            if (fullNameMode === "track" || fullNameMode === "both") {
              segmentTrack["first_name"] = fName;
              if (lName) {
                segmentTrack["last_name"] = lName;
              }
            }
          }
          return;
        }

        // 2.3 Email
        if (isEmail) {
          const emailMode = input.getAttribute("data-segment-mode") || "identify";
          if (emailMode !== "none") {
            if (emailMode === "identify" || emailMode === "both") {
              segmentIdentify["email"] = value;
            }
            if (emailMode === "track" || emailMode === "both") {
              segmentTrack["email"] = value;
            }
          }
          return;
        }

        // 2.4 First name
        if (identity === "first_name") {
          const firstNameMode = input.getAttribute("data-segment-mode") || "identify";
          if (firstNameMode !== "none") {
            if (firstNameMode === "identify" || firstNameMode === "both") {
              segmentIdentify["first_name"] = value;
            }
            if (firstNameMode === "track" || firstNameMode === "both") {
              segmentTrack["first_name"] = value;
            }
          }
          return;
        }

        // 2.5 Last name
        if (identity === "last_name") {
          const lastNameMode = input.getAttribute("data-segment-mode") || "identify";
          if (lastNameMode !== "none") {
            if (lastNameMode === "identify" || lastNameMode === "both") {
              segmentIdentify["last_name"] = value;
            }
            if (lastNameMode === "track" || lastNameMode === "both") {
              segmentTrack["last_name"] = value;
            }
          }
          return;
        }

        // 3) GENERIČKA Segment logika
        if (segmentMode !== "none") {
          if (segmentMode === "track" || segmentMode === "both") {
            segmentTrack[segmentKey] = value;
          }
          if (segmentMode === "identify" || segmentMode === "both") {
            segmentIdentify[segmentKey] = value;
          }
        }
      });

      // --- METADATA + REFERRER ---

      const metadata = {
        GA4_client_ID: gaId
      };

      if (formNameAttr) {
        metadata.form_name = formNameAttr;
      }

      if (!disableFormUrl) {
        metadata.form_url = window.location.href;
      }

      const referrerData = getReferrerIds(window.location.search);

      // --- SEGMENT ---

      if (window.analytics) {
        let finalTrack = { ...metadata, ...segmentTrack };

        // IonQ-style minimal event → samo meta
        if (minimalSegment) {
          finalTrack = { ...metadata };
        }

        const categoryAttr = form.getAttribute("data-segment-category");
        if (categoryAttr) {
          finalTrack.category = categoryAttr;
        }

        const labelAttr = form.getAttribute("data-segment-label");
        if (labelAttr) {
          finalTrack.label = labelAttr;
        }

        if (formMode === "identify") {
          delete finalTrack.form_name;
        }
        if (urlMode === "identify") {
          delete finalTrack.form_url;
        }

        log("[Segment] Track:", finalTrack);
        window.analytics.track(eventName, finalTrack);

        if (shouldIdentify && segmentIdentify.email) {
          const finalIdentify = {
            ...metadata,
            ...referrerData,
            ...segmentIdentify
          };

          if (formMode === "track") {
            delete finalIdentify.form_name;
          }
          if (urlMode === "track") {
            delete finalIdentify.form_url;
          }

          log("[Segment] Identify:", finalIdentify);
          window.analytics.identify(finalIdentify);
        }
      }
    }

  });
</script></div><div class="hidden-css w-embed"><style>
